evals.name: bootstrap-cross-sysroot
evals.prompt: We've been versioning our sensor-daemon manually in a spreadsheet until now and I want to switch to a proper semantic versioning mechanism. The repo is at {REPO} — it's a CMake project cross-compiled for aarch64 (the toolchain file is in the repo, sysroot included). Our current version is 2.1.0. Set up the versioning mechanism for this project so that future releases can be computed automatically.
evals.expected_output: dependency.md created with '2.1.0+<count>.g<hash>' alone on line 1, a YAML snapshot with target.mode=cross-sysroot, aarch64 triple, sysroot path, cxx_standard 14, spdlog 1.9.2 and opencv_core 4.1.1 discovered from the sysroot; file committed; tag v2.1.0 created; no version bump performed.

evals.name: mixed-fix-feat-release
evals.prompt: Cut a new release of logtool at {REPO}. The project already uses our dependency.md + git-tag versioning mechanism. Work out the new version number and produce all the release artifacts.
evals.expected_output: Since v1.2.3 there is one fix: and one feat: commit and no dependency changes -> leftmost wins -> MINOR -> 1.3.0 with PATCH reset to 0. dependency.md rewritten (line 1 = 1.3.0+3.g<hash>), release_note_1.3.0*.md generated but NOT committed (gitignored), CHANGELOG.md appended and committed, tag v1.3.0 on the release commit.

evals.name: dep-only-update-release
evals.prompt: Time to release fooapp at {REPO}. Since the last release the only thing that happened is that we dropped in an updated build of the vendored libfoo — no source changes at all. What should the new version be? Generate the release artifacts as well.
evals.expected_output: libfoo went 1.2.0 -> 1.4.0 with the same SONAME (libfoo.so.1) and zero app code impact -> PATCH -> 0.9.2. dependency.md updated (line 1 = 0.9.2+2.g<hash>, libfoo version 1.4.0), release note generated uncommitted, CHANGELOG appended, tag v0.9.2.
